sources.notes: Official tutorial tightens the candidate-reduction strategy using binary-search style splits and probability analysis.

annotation.summary: Find the hidden singleton under stricter interactive limits.
annotation.constraints: The harder version requires nearly logarithmic query complexity with controlled error probability.
annotation.core_idea: Use a binary-search-like candidate reduction: test carefully chosen halves/subsets so that every answer removes a constant fraction of candidates. Randomization or repeated checks handle unreliable/noisy structure, while the final small candidate set is verified directly.
annotation.complexity: O(log n) or O(log n log log n) queries depending on amplification constants.

solution_variants.name: probabilistic-binary-search
solution_variants.summary: Maintain a candidate interval/set and test halves with enough repetitions to keep the hidden singleton with high probability.
solution_variants.complexity: O(log n log log n) queries

tags.evidence: The solution is an adaptive query strategy for an interactive hidden element.
The candidate set is reduced by binary-search style half tests.
The correctness depends on success-probability bounds and amplification.
